title: Nigerian Exchange Group, SEC Engage Indian Counterparts to Boost Financial Cooperation - FinancialEDGE
description: The Nigerian Exchange Group and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) have taken a significant step to strengthen financial cooperation with India. html:
The Nigerian Exchange Group and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) have taken a significant step to strengthen financial cooperation with India.

Recently, a high-level delegation comprising Alhaji (Dr.) Umaru Kwairanga, Chairman of the Nigerian Exchange Group, Mr. Mairiga Katuka, Chairman of the Board of the SEC, and Dr. Emomotimi Agama, Director General of the SEC, engaged with the National Stock Exchange (NSE) of India and key players in India’s financial ecosystem.

This strategic meeting aimed to foster collaboration and explore opportunities for growth between Nigeria’s financial sector and India’s.

By sharing knowledge, expertise, and best practices, both countries can enhance their financial markets and create mutually beneficial partnerships.

The outcome of this engagement is expected to have a positive impact on Nigeria’s financial landscape, promoting economic growth and development.
